big_picture: India is facing a significant oil crisis due to the attack on gas fields in Iran and Qatar, which has led to a surge in oil prices, with Brent jumping 3% to above $110. The country is also experiencing disruptions in its trade with the Middle East, affecting its gems and jewellery exports. To mitigate the impact, India has decided to extend its pulse deal with Myanmar for 5 years to secure 'dal' supplies amid global supply chain shocks. The government has also offered insurance support to exporters as war risk drives premiums. In other news, the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has released its first list of 88 candidates for the Assam polls, with Chief Minister Sarma to contest from Jalukbari.

articles.title: Place of Supply of Services Rules Under GST
articles.summary: The Government of India has defined rules for determining the place of supply of services under the Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime. This is crucial for determining GST liability. The rules aim to clarify whether a transaction is intra-State or inter-State, which has significant implications for businesses and the economy.
articles.raw_summary: GST liability depends on correct determination of place of supply. The rules define whether a transaction is intra-State or inter-State. The post Place of Supply of Services Rules Under GST appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Sec 80P(2)(d) Allowed on Interest from Co-op Bank – ITAT Follows Settled View
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) has ruled that interest earned by a co-operative society from deposits with a co-operative bank qualifies for deduction under Section 80P(2)(d). This decision follows the settled view and reverses an addition made by the Commissioner of Income Tax (CPC). The ruling is significant for co-operative societies and banks in India.

This ensures farmers receive fair returns and prevents distress sales.

articles.title: Indian exporters facing shipment delays amid West Asia crisis; govt sets up inter-ministerial group
articles.summary: Indian exporters are grappling with significant shipment delays. The ongoing West Asia conflict is disrupting trade routes, impacting the movement of goods and increasing logistics costs. The Indian government has formed an inter-ministerial group to address these challenges. Measures are being implemented to support affected exporters and manage returning cargo. This situation affects key sectors and is being closely monitored.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu surpasses Gujarat, Maharashtra to become India's top textile exporter
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu has become India's top state for textile exports. Shipments reached USD 7,997.17 million in fiscal year 2024-25. This marks a significant 29.12 percent increase over the past four years. Gujarat and Maharashtra follow Tamil Nadu in textile export value. The state's success is attributed to planned government actions and multifaceted growth initiatives.

articles.title: Rs 1 lakh crore wiped off! HDFC Bank shares slump 9%, set to record worst day since Covid crash
articles.url: Rs 1 lakh crore wiped off! HDFC Bank share price slumps 9%, set to record worst day since Covid crash
articles.summary: HDFC Bank shares plunged, erasing nearly ₹1 lakh crore in market value and marking their worst fall since the 2020 COVID crash. The decline followed the resignation of chairman Atanu Chakraborty, who cited value and ethics concerns. The bank said there were no other reasons for his exit, and Keki Mistry has been appointed interim chairman for three months.

articles.title: Rupee hits new low; dwindling forex reserves and dollar surge fuel currency crisis: Naveen Mathur

articles.title: Legislative Assembly Speaker doesn’t enjoy constitutional immunity from judicial scrutiny under Article 122 and 212
articles.summary: The Supreme Court held that the Legislative Assembly Speaker does not enjoy constitutional immunity from judicial scrutiny under Article 122 and 212 of the Constitution. This ruling means that the Speaker's actions can be subject to judicial review. The court's decision is significant for the governance of India's legislative bodies.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court held that Legislative Assembly Speakers acts as a Tribunal under the Tenth Schedule to the Constitution and doesn’t enjoy the constitutional immunity from judicial scrutiny under Article 122 and 212 of the Constitution. articles.title: Govt mulls authorising more ministries to issue content takedown orders
articles.summary: Discussions are on with Defence, Ministry of External Affairs, and Ministry of Home Affairs, government sources said adding that once decided, the changes can be brought through tweaks in IT Rules itself. Section 69A of the IT Act, 2000, empowers the Centre to block public access to online content, websites, apps, or social media posts in the interest of national security, sovereignty and public order.

articles.title: India's privatisation drive derails because of weak investor interest
articles.summary: India faces challenges in its privatisation program. Three planned sales are under consideration for shelving due to weak investor interest. This follows the failed sale of IDBI Bank. Dwindling buyer interest, high valuations, and geopolitical uncertainty are impacting the divestment drive. The government's ambitious plan to exit most sectors is encountering fresh setbacks.
articles.raw_summary: India faces challenges in its privatisation program. Three planned sales are under consideration for shelving due to weak investor interest. The government's ambitious plan to exit most sectors is encountering fresh setbacks.

articles.title: Parliamentary panel urges a 'golden share' strategy to protect PSU autonomy if state stakes fall below 51%
articles.summary: A parliamentary panel has urged the government to finalize a 'golden share' strategy to protect PSU autonomy. The panel also recommended enhancing MoU frameworks, prioritizing vacant officer positions, and developing a roadmap for InvITs and REITs. These measures aim to bridge revenue gaps and optimize debt management for economic stability and growth.
articles.raw_summary: A parliamentary panel urged the government to finalize a 'golden share' strategy to protect PSU autonomy as state stakes fall below 51%. Recommendations also include enhancing MoU frameworks, prioritizing vacant officer positions, and developing a roadmap for InvITs and REITs. The committee stressed bridging revenue gaps and optimizing debt management for economic stability and growth.

articles.title: India's data centre capacity grew fourfold to more than 1500 MW in 5 years: IT Ministry
articles.summary: Data centre capacity in the country has grown from 375 Megawatt (MW) in 2020 to more than 1500 MW till 2025, according to a statement released by the Ministry of Electronics   IT. In line with Prime Minister Narendra Modi's vision, the Government is democratising the development and usage of technology. The focus is on facilitating the establishment of data centres in the country, which will help strengthen digital infrastructure and improve delivery of digital services across various sectors.

articles.title: Statement on HDFC Bank Limited
articles.summary: The RBI has approved a transition arrangement for HDFC Bank's Part Time Chairman position. HDFC Bank is a Domestic Systemically Important Bank with sound financials and a professionally run board. The RBI has found no material concerns regarding the bank's conduct or governance.
articles.raw_summary: The Reserve Bank has taken note of the recent developments in HDFC Bank. A transition arrangement as requested by the Bank has been approved by Reserve Bank as regards the position of Part Time Chairman of the Bank. HDFC Bank is a Domestic Systemically Important Bank (D-SIB) with sound financials, professionally run board and competent management team. Basis our periodical assessment, there are no material concerns on record as regards its conduct or governance. The bank remains well-capitalized

This trend may prompt policy responses from the government or regulators, given its impact on fuel costs and broader economic stability.

The survey underscores the need for policy interventions to address these challenges.

articles.title: Pensioners’ demands echoed: Parliamentary panel seeks hike in minimum EPS payout, say Rs 1000 insufficient
articles.summary: A parliamentary panel has urged the government to review the Employees' Pension Scheme 1995's minimum monthly pension, which is currently Rs 1,000. Pensioners have demanded an increase to Rs 7,500. The panel also recommended improvements in social security for contractual and gig workers.
articles.raw_summary: A parliamentary panel has urged an immediate review of the Employees' Pension Scheme 1995's minimum monthly pension. The current Rs 1,000 is deemed insufficient for basic needs. Pensioners have demanded an increase to Rs 7,500. The committee also recommended improvements for contractual and gig workers' social security.

articles.title: Bank-NBFC co-lending pacts may be expanded to aid MSMEs
articles.summary: A parliamentary panel has recommended expanding co-lending partnerships between banks and non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) to help micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) access cheaper credit. The committee also proposed listing profitable regional rural banks and strengthening state-run general insurers. It highlighted a credit gap for small businesses and declining insurance penetration, suggesting measures like climate risk insurance and using business correspondents to reach rur
articles.raw_summary: A parliamentary panel suggests boosting bank and NBFC co-lending to help MSMEs access cheaper credit. It also proposes listing profitable regional rural banks and reinforcing state-run general insurers. The committee highlighted a significant credit gap for small businesses and a decline in insurance penetration. Recommendations include developing climate risk insurance and using business correspondents to reach rural areas.

articles.title: Easier FDI norms notified for border country investors
articles.summary: India has updated its foreign direct investment policy. Investments from countries sharing a land border now face stricter government scrutiny. Overseas companies with up to 10% Chinese shareholding can invest via the automatic route, subject to limits. However, entities registered in China or other bordering nations require government approval. This change aims to regulate investments from neighboring countries.
articles.raw_summary: India has updated its foreign direct investment policy. Investments from countries sharing a land border now face stricter government scrutiny. Overseas companies with up to 10% Chinese shareholding can invest via the automatic route, subject to limits. However, entities registered in China or other bordering nations require government approval. This change aims to regulate investments from neighboring countries.

articles.title: Govt imposes import curbs on silver jewellery studded with diamonds
articles.summary: The Indian government has imposed import restrictions on silver jewellery studded with inexpensive diamonds, effective immediately until June 30, 2026. The Directorate General of Foreign Trade announced the move, shifting from unrestricted to restricted imports to curb silver imports from ASEAN nations. The policy aims to regulate imports of such jewellery.
articles.raw_summary: New import restrictions are now in effect for silver jewellery featuring inexpensive diamonds, with the government limiting these imports until June 30, 2026. This policy revision, moving from free to restricted, aims to curb silver imports from ASEAN nations. The Directorate General of Foreign Trade announced the immediate implementation of these curbs.

articles.title: NCERT textbook row: Priyanka Chaturvedi flags ‘judicial dictatorship’ in RS after SC bar on authors
articles.summary: Priyanka Chaturvedi criticized the Supreme Court's direction to the Centre and states to dissociate from academics who drafted a chapter on the judiciary in NCERT textbooks, calling it 'judicial dictatorship.' The court's order has sparked debate over judicial overreach and its impact on educational content and academic freedom.

articles.title: Supreme Court says paid menstrual leave will end women’s careers, calls PIL a bid to ‘instil fear’
articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a Public Interest Litigation (PIL) seeking paid menstrual leave, stating that such policies could negatively impact women's careers. The court said the PIL aimed to 'instil fear' by suggesting women are not on par with men during menstruation. The ruling has implications for workplace policies and gender equality in India.
articles.raw_summary: Dismissing PIL, SC said: 'This is only to create impression in young women that you have some natural issues, and are not at par with males, and you cannot work like them during a particular time.'

articles.title: SC sets record straight for OBC quota—social status, not paycheck must be measure for ‘creamy layer’
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that social status, not just parental income, should determine 'creamy layer' status for OBC reservations. This decision impacts students who were denied benefits based solely on their parents' salaries. The ruling clarifies eligibility for government-backed educational opportunities under affirmative action policies.
articles.raw_summary: Students involved in legal challenges were successful Civil Services Examination candidates whose OBC (non-creamy layer) status was rejected based on parents’ salaries.

This collaboration will enable Japanese companies to partner with cities globally to design and implement solutions for urban challenges. The proposed model will be backed by government support.

articles.title: Nazara Technologies to acquire 50% stake in Spain-based Bluetile, BestPlay for $100 million
articles.summary: Gaming firm Nazara Technologies will acquire a 50% controlling stake in Spain’s Bluetile Games and BestPlay Systems for $100.3 million (Rs 918 crore), its largest deal yet. The move will be done via Nazara Technologies UK. The transaction also includes performance-linked earnouts of up to $98.2 million, contingent on achieving revenue and profitability targets between 2027 and 2029.
articles.raw_summary: Gaming firm Nazara Technologies will acquire a 50% controlling stake in Spain’s Bluetile Games and BestPlay Systems for $100.3 million (Rs 918 crore), its largest deal yet. The move will be done via Nazara Technologies UK. The transaction also includes performance-linked earnouts of up to $98.2 million, contingent on achieving revenue and profitability targets between 2027 and 2029.

articles.title: Chipmakers in Malaysia monitoring risks from helium supply disruptions, association says
articles.summary: Helium prices have risen sharply due to disruptions in natural gas processing in Qatar. This may impact industries like semiconductors and medical imaging, which rely on helium as a byproduct of LNG processing.
articles.raw_summary: Helium prices have risen sharply due ‌to the disruption ⁠of ⁠natural gas processing in Qatar by the U.S.-Israel war against Iran. Helium - ​critical for industries such as semiconductors and medical imaging - is a ​byproduct of LNG processing, and any slowdown in output is expected to affect global supplies.

articles.title: Early summer heat drives D2C and quick commerce demand for essentials
articles.summary: The early summer heat is expected to drive demand for essentials on quick commerce platforms, with a higher share of sales coming from impulse purchases. This may lead to increased demand for delivery services and potentially impact trade policies. The article highlights the potential implications of this trend on the economy.
articles.raw_summary: Typically, the peak season for brands selling beverages and ice creams, this summer is expected to see a higher share of sales coming from quick commerce platforms, as these products are often impulse purchases that consumers prefer to have delivered quickly.

articles.title: IPO-bound Udaan to kickstart reverse flip to India in weeks: CEO Vaibhav Gupta
articles.summary: B2B ecommerce firm Udaan plans to shift its headquarters from Singapore to India as part of a reverse merger, aiming for an IPO within 9–18 months. After scaling back operations to focus on essentials, groceries, and Horeca360, the company has cut losses and narrowed its footprint from 80 to 16 cities, serving 200,000 shops.
articles.raw_summary: B2B ecommerce firm Udaan plans to shift its headquarters from Singapore to India as part of a reverse merger, aiming for an IPO within 9–18 months. After scaling back operations to focus on essentials, groceries, and Horeca360, the company has cut losses and narrowed its footprint from 80 to 16 cities, serving 200,000 shops.

articles.title: NPCI counts on Bharat, Northeast to accelerate UPI adoption
articles.summary: As the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) grapples with a moderation in growth of the Unified Payments Interface, it is turning its focus to the Hindi heartland and northeastern states to drive the next phase of adoption. NPCI is nudging fintech firms to prioritise states such as Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh, along with the northeastern region, where penetration remains relatively low.

NPCI is nudging fintech firms to prioritise states such as Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh, along with the northeastern region, where penetration remains relatively low.

articles.title: Rising component cost to keep hardware prices elevated in near term, Lenovo executive says
articles.summary: Lenovo executive says component shortages will keep hardware prices high in the near term. This is due to global shortages of key components like memory chips. The impact is on the artificial intelligence computing ecosystem.
articles.raw_summary: Shortages of key components such as memory chips are pushing up costs across the artificial intelligence computing ecosystem globally and are likely to keep hardware prices elevated in the near term, a senior executive at Chinese technology company Lenovo said.

articles.title: Drone-led logistics may reduce need for standalone labs: Narayana Health’s Devi Shetty
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based hospital chain Narayana Health has partnered with drone startup Airbound to transport blood samples and units between its facilities. This initiative aims to reduce the need for standalone labs. The partnership has completed over 700 incident-free flights, carrying up to 40 samples per trip.
articles.raw_summary: The Bengaluru-based hospital chain has partnered with drone startup Airbound, also based in Bengaluru, to transport blood samples and blood units between its facilities in the city, with pilots underway since last year. Meanwhile, Airbound has completed over 700 drone incident-free flights across 54 consecutive days, carrying up to 40 samples per trip.

articles.title: Mastercard to buy stablecoin infra firm BVNK for up to $1.8 billion
articles.summary: Mastercard is set to acquire BVNK, a stablecoin payments infrastructure company, for up to $1.8 billion. This deal signals Mastercard's move into digital asset ecosystems. The acquisition aims to connect fiat and stablecoins across major blockchains, positioning Mastercard at the forefront of digital currency services.
articles.raw_summary: Mastercard is set to acquire BVNK, a stablecoin payments infrastructure company. The deal, valued up to $1.8 billion, signals Mastercard's move into digital asset ecosystems. BVNK's platform connects fiat and stablecoins across major blockchains. This acquisition is expected to be finalized by the end of 2026. Mastercard aims to be at the forefront of digital currency services.

articles.title: Half of new-age listings fall below issue price amid market volatility
articles.summary: New-age firms listed in India over the past year are struggling due to market volatility and US-Iran tensions. Many of these firms are trading below their issue price. This is impacting investor confidence and profitability concerns.
articles.raw_summary: New-age firms listed over the past year remain under pressure from US-Iran tensions and volatile markets, with many trading below issue price. In addition, PhonePe delaying its IPO may impact others. While a few stocks show gains, several continue to struggle with profitability concerns and weak investor confidence.

articles.summary: Menon will join Steadview Capital from Fidelity-backed Eight Roads Ventures, which he left earlier this year. He was a principal at Eight Roads and led early-stage investments across software, enterprise, consumer and fintech spaces at the firm.

articles.title: West Asia in turmoil, govt looks to clear trade routes to South Asia
articles.summary: India is focusing on increasing exports to South Asian nations like Bangladesh, Nepal, and Sri Lanka. This strategic shift comes as exports to West Asia face potential decline. The government is addressing trade barriers including tariffs and logistics. Efforts are underway to resolve payment issues and enhance trade promotion. Bangladesh remains India's largest export market in South Asia.

articles.title: Space for global growth to remain contested; greater intra-state coordination needed to counter external headwinds: CEA
articles.summary: Chief Economic Advisor V Anantha Nageswaran urged for enhanced intra-state coordination, advocating for an "entrepreneurial state" to navigate global economic challenges. He stressed a shift from extraction to development, emphasizing innovation, risk-taking, and outcome-focused governance. Nageswaran highlighted the need for participatory governance, viewing it as a feedback loop to build trust and accelerate economic scaling for India.

articles.title: India’s tax coffers swell as direct tax collections cross ₹22.8 lakh crore, up 7.2%
articles.summary: India's tax landscape has experienced a remarkable upturn, with net collections reaching an impressive ₹22.80 lakh crore as of March 17, 2026, marking a notable 7.19% growth compared to the previous year. The surge can be attributed to robust performances in both corporate and non-corporate tax sectors. While gross collections soared, the trend of refunds showed a decline.

articles.title: CCEA approves Rs 1,718.56 crore funding to CCI for cotton procurement operations
articles.summary: The government has approved Rs 1,718.56 crore for cotton procurement. This funding will support cotton farmers across India. The Cotton Corporation of India will undertake these operations. This move aims to strengthen farmer welfare and advance the Aatmanirbhar Bharat vision. Cotton is a vital cash crop for millions of Indian farmers and supports many allied activities.

articles.title: India launches anti-dumping probe into ethyl chloroformate imports from China
articles.summary: India has launched an anti-dumping investigation into ethyl chloroformate imports from China. A domestic producer alleged unfair pricing practices. Upcoming free trade agreements will unlock significant opportunities for Indian businesses.

articles.title: Rs 39.48 lakh cr sanctioned so far under PM-MUDRA, NPAs at around 2 pc: FM Sitharaman
articles.summary: The Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana has empowered numerous small entrepreneurs. Over Rs 39 lakh crore has been sanctioned since its launch. Non-performing assets remain low at approximately 2 percent. The scheme supports new and women entrepreneurs significantly. Banks are actively recovering outstanding loans through various measures.

articles.title: India oilmeal exports hit by US-Iran conflict, Red Sea disruptions
articles.summary: Escalating US-Iran tensions are disrupting India's oilmeal exports, with significant portions to the Middle East and Europe facing logistics challenges due to Red Sea and Strait of Hormuz instability. Shipping detours and increased costs are impacting overall export volumes, while a surge in Chinese demand for Indian rapeseed meal faces renewed competition following tariff suspensions.

Shipping detours and increased costs are impacting overall export volumes, while a surge in Chinese demand for Indian rapeseed meal faces renewed competition following tariff suspensions.

articles.title: India growth outlook dims as oil tops $100, gas crisis deepens
articles.summary: A distant war is impacting India's economy. Soaring oil prices and gas shortages are affecting homes and businesses. This energy crisis threatens economic growth and raises inflation concerns. Industries are halting operations. The situation is creating strain for everyday citizens and impacting employment. The external sector faces significant risks as well.

articles.title: What's key to India's forex defence? Patra suggests it's $1 trillion buffer
articles.summary: Former RBI Deputy Governor Michael Patra advocates for India to build foreign exchange reserves to at least $1 trillion. This substantial buffer is crucial for market intervention, covering external debt obligations and cushioning against portfolio investment outflows. Recent sharp declines highlight the pressure on reserves amid global uncertainties.

articles.title: Biz chamber seeks RBI relief for exporters hit by West Asia disruptions
articles.summary: Indian exporters are facing significant challenges due to global shipping route disruptions. The Bharat Chamber of Commerce has requested the Reserve Bank of India to provide crucial banking support. This includes enhanced working capital, flexible credit, and extended repayment periods. The aim is to safeguard exporters from financial strain and ensure continued trade flow amidst these global uncertainties.

articles.title: India underreported rice, wheat price support: US, others to WTO
articles.summary: The WTO landscape is heating up as India encounters objections from the US, Australia, Paraguay, and Ukraine regarding its agricultural support practices. These nations contend that India's reported market price support for rice and wheat is misleadingly low, pointing to substantial discrepancies.

articles.title: India’s wholesale inflation rises to 11-month high of 2.13% in February
articles.summary: India's wholesale price inflation reached an 11-month high of 2.13% in February, driven by increased costs of primary goods like food and non-food articles. Economists anticipate further acceleration due to rising input costs and ongoing geopolitical tensions, with manufacturing products also showing a slight increase.

Economists anticipate further acceleration due to rising input costs and ongoing geopolitical tensions, with manufacturing products also showing a slight increase.

articles.title: Trade deficit nearly 2x in Feb; exports face battlefield ahead
articles.summary: India's trade deficit widened significantly in February, reaching $27 billion. This increase was driven by a substantial rise in imports, especially gold and silver. Exports experienced a minor decrease. The government plans to introduce measures to support exporters facing logistical challenges. The trade gap with China also saw an increase during the fiscal year.

The trade gap with China also saw an increase during the fiscal year.

articles.title: India-US trade deal to be signed after new US tariff framework is finalised
articles.summary: India-US trade deal signing is contingent on a new tariff framework, with discussions ongoing regarding non-tariff barriers and Section 232 tariffs. The agreement will also address US Section 301 tariffs, with finalization awaiting Washington's restoration of global tariff architecture post-Supreme Court ruling.

The agreement will also address US Section 301 tariffs, with finalization awaiting Washington's restoration of global tariff architecture post-Supreme Court ruling.

articles.title: Revamped Stand-Up India scheme soon: Nirmala Sitharaman
articles.summary: Replying to a supplementary question in the Lok Sabha, Sitharaman said the modified programme would incorporate lessons from the earlier scheme, which was discontinued in April 2025 after nine years. "With studies done by Niti Aayog and others, this scheme is being redrafted and soon the Cabinet will be given a proposal with which we will launch a new scheme to continue with Stand-Up India, which benefitted SC/ST and women all over the country," she said.

articles.title: Exporters, freight forwarders seek government help as war surcharges on cargo surge
articles.summary: Amid escalating geopolitical tensions, Indian exporters and freight forwarders are appealing for government support. Shipping lines worldwide are imposing significant war-risk surcharges on Middle Eastern shipments, often doubling the previously established rates.

articles.summary: The PHD Chamber of Commerce and Industry has approached the Reserve Bank of India. They are seeking increased credit for small businesses. The chamber also wants the RBI to resolve issues with letters of credit. Inconsistent handling of document discrepancies by banks is a concern. Unfair practices by rating agencies are also highlighted. Clearer guidelines for banks are requested.

articles.title: India’s horticulture production to increase marginally in 2025-26: First Advance Estimate
articles.summary: India's horticulture production is set to remain steady in 2025-26. Vegetable output is expected to rise, driven by onions and potatoes. Fruit production will see a slight increase. Flower production may see a minor dip. Spice output is projected to grow significantly, with gains in cumin, ginger, garlic, and turmeric. This sector is crucial for boosting farmer incomes.

This sector is crucial for boosting farmer incomes.

articles.title: Govt approves additional 87,587 tonnes sugar export quota for 2025-26
articles.summary: In a significant move, India's food ministry has greenlit an additional quota of 87,587 tonnes of sugar exports for the upcoming 2025-26 season, responding to demands from sugar mills. These mills are required to complete the exports by June 30, 2026, with those meeting a threshold of 70 percent allowed to ship the remaining sugar by September 30, 2026.

These mills are required to complete the exports by June 30, 2026, with those meeting a threshold of 70 percent allowed to ship the remaining sugar by September 30, 2026.

articles.title: DPIIT notifies changes in FDI norms for China, other land border sharing countries
articles.summary: India has taken a bold step toward enhancing its investment landscape by relaxing restrictions on foreign investments. Companies with up to ten percent Chinese shareholding can now invest seamlessly, a notable change that leaves out entities from neighboring countries, who still require prior authorization.

It highlighted UPL Global's backend moat that assured supply from UPL Limited's Superform manufacturing, R D integration, and specialty chemicals.

articles.title: India inks terms of reference with Philippines, Maldives for trade pact talks
articles.summary: India has initiated negotiations for trade agreements with the Philippines and the Maldives, signing terms of reference for a preferential trade agreement (PTA) with the former and a free trade agreement (FTA) with the latter. These moves come as India reviews its existing trade pacts with ASEAN and Korea, and pursues new agreements with several other nations and blocs.

articles.title: Inflation to stay sticky, Jahangir Aziz rules out Fed rate cuts in 2026
articles.summary: Economist Jahangir Aziz believes that inflation will remain high and the US Federal Reserve will not cut interest rates in 2026. This could lead to demand destruction and impact global markets. The article highlights the complex risks facing global markets, including geopolitical tensions and fragmented oil prices.
articles.raw_summary: Global markets face complex risks from geopolitical tensions and fragmented oil prices. Economist Jahangir Aziz highlights that Brent crude is not the sole indicator. He believes the US Federal Reserve will not cut rates this year, with a potential hike in 2027. Persistent inflation could lead to demand destruction. Investors must look beyond surface-level signals.

articles.title: Is your insurance adequate? Here’s a simple checklist to find out
articles.summary: Many policyholders in India are underinsured, leading to financial strain in case of unforeseen events. Reviewing health, home, cyber, and motor insurance covers is advised to ensure they match current needs and asset values. Adequate insurance provides a crucial safety net against financial strain.

articles.title: Rajputana Stainless shares make a muted debut, list at 2% premium over IPO price
articles.summary: Rajputana Stainless made a muted debut, listing at Rs 123.95 on BSE, a 1.6% premium over its Rs 122 issue price, while NSE saw it at par. The Rs 255 crore IPO, combining a fresh issue and offer-for-sale, raised funds for expansion and debt repayment. Subscriptions were modest, with strong institutional interest but weak retail participation at 0.27 times.

articles.title: 'A buy-on-dips pick': Why HDFC Bank is getting backing from analysts despite management blip
articles.summary: HDFC Bank shares fell after its chairman's sudden exit. Analysts see this as a buying opportunity due to undervaluation. The bank's strong track record is expected to mitigate any governance concerns.
articles.raw_summary: HDFC Bank shares fell after chairman Atanu Chakraborty’s sudden exit, wiping out significant market value. However, analysts largely see the drop as a buying opportunity, with valuations entering a “deep value” zone. While a slight governance discount may emerge, most experts do not view the resignation as a fundamental concern and remain confident in the bank’s strong track record.

articles.title: Silver drops Rs 4,000, gold slips Rs 1,300 as US Fed’s hawkish tone clouds rate cut trajectory. What should your strategy be?
articles.summary: Gold and silver prices declined in India after the US Federal Reserve maintained rates and signaled a hawkish stance. This may lead to volatility in the market, and analysts advise caution. Investors are waiting for stability before taking fresh positions in bullion.
articles.raw_summary: Gold and silver prices declined on 19 March, 2026 after the US Federal Reserve maintained rates and signalled a hawkish stance. While domestic prices fell sharply, global gold and silver saw modest gains. Analysts expect continued volatility and advise caution, recommending investors wait for stability before taking fresh positions in bullion.

articles.title: Infosys, TCS, other IT stocks fall up to 2% as US Fed's hawkish tone clouds rate cut trajectory
articles.summary: Indian IT stocks fell up to 2% after the US Federal Reserve kept policy rates unchanged, citing persistent inflation concerns. Higher energy prices and geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, coupled with tariff impacts, are pushing up inflation projections. This decision, along with existing investor worries about AI's impact on demand, contributed to a broader market downturn.

articles.title: Enterprise procurement platform Aerchain raises $13 million from Pavestone VC, IndiaMART
articles.summary: Aerchain, an enterprise procurement platform, has raised $13 million in funding led by Pavestone VC and IndiaMART. The platform automates procurement tasks and can reduce task completion times from days to hours. This development may have implications for the Indian startup ecosystem and business operations.
articles.raw_summary: Aerchain raised $13 million led by Pavestone VC with IndiaMART joining. Founded by Harsha Kadimisetty and Himavanth Jasti, Aerchain’s platform is designed to automate a chunk of procurement tasks such as intake routing, policy enforcement, approval workflows, and vendor sourcing. The company said the system can reduce task completion times from days to hours.

articles.title: Real estate AI platform VerbaFlo raises $7 million from Pi Labs, Haatch, Navigate Ventures
articles.summary: VerbaFlo, a real estate AI platform, has raised $7 million in funding led by Pi Labs. The platform aims to deliver operational benefits, including faster leasing and improved resident experience. This development may have implications for the Indian real estate sector, but it does not directly involve government policy or regulation.
articles.raw_summary: VerbaFlo raised $7 million led by Pi Labs with investors Haatch, Navigate Ventures, Old College Capital and family offices, taking total funding to $9 million. Faisal Butt, founder and managing partner at Pi Labs, said the platform's impact is immediate and can deliver operational benefits, including faster leasing, lower operational friction, and improved resident experience.

articles.title: 47% of sitting MLAs in poll-bound West Bengal face criminal cases: ADR
articles.summary: A report by the Association for Democratic Reforms (ADR) reveals that 47% of sitting MLAs in West Bengal face criminal cases, with 37% facing serious charges. The findings come ahead of the state's elections on April 23 and 29, with results declared on May 4. The report also notes that 52% of MLAs are crorepatis and 15% are women. The data raises questions about governance and electoral integrity in the state.
articles.raw_summary: Nearly half of West Bengal's legislators have criminal cases, says ADR. Elections are on April 23 and 29, results on May 4. 47% face charges, 37% serious. 52% are crorepatis, 15% women.

articles.title: Rajya Sabha Election 2026: What is cross-voting
articles.summary: Cross-voting in Rajya Sabha elections refers to when a Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) votes for a candidate from a different party, contrary to their own party's directive or candidate preferences.

Internal discussions aim to leverage the charisma of actor Vijay and the strength of seasoned parties to create electoral synergy. The outcome could impact Tamil Nadu's political landscape ahead of elections.

articles.title: Memory chip shortage to continue until 2030, says SK Group chairman
articles.summary: SK Group chairman Chey Tae-won predicts a global shortage of memory chips will persist for the next 4-5 years, with supply unable to meet demand until at least 2030. The shortage is driven by dominant players like SK Hynix, Samsung, and Micron struggling to keep up with demand. This could impact industries reliant on semiconductors, including India's growing electronics and tech sectors. The situation may prompt policy measures to address supply chain vulnerabilities or boost domestic production
articles.raw_summary: Chey Tae-won, SK Group chairman, predicts a global memory chip shortage for 4-5 more years. SK Hynix, Samsung, and Micron control supply but can't meet demand until 2030.

articles.title: ETtech Explainer: How does Meta’s decision to kill end-to-end encryption on Instagram affect you?
articles.summary: Meta has decided to remove end-to-end encryption on Instagram. This means that messages sent on the platform may no longer be secure. The decision may have implications for data privacy in India, where encryption is a growing concern.
articles.raw_summary: End-to-end encryption is a communications process that ensures that only the sender and recipient can access the contents of a message. The broader push for encryption within Meta dates back to 2016, when WhatsApp began rolling out end-to-end encryption across its services.
